rulings.Case Title: BASILIO UDARBE ET AL VS. PREMA P. THEKKEK ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Conditional Certification Of A Settlement Class; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2024, Line 2. PLAINTIFF BASILIO UDARBE , INDIVIDUALLY AND ON BEHALF OF ALL OTHER SIMILARLY SITUATED PERSONS, JARMILA ROMANOVA, AND BRANDON THIBEAULT's Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Action Settlement And Conditional Certification Of A Settlement Class. Plaintiffs' unopposed motion for preliminary approval of class action settlement is granted; hearing required. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC. ET AL VS. NKECHI EMERUWA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2024, Line 4. PLAINTIFF BUDGET RENT A CAR SYSTEM, INC., AND PV HOLDING CORPORATION's Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint. The Budget plaintiffs' motion for leave to file first amended complaint is granted. "That trial courts are to liberally permit such amendments, at any stage of the proceeding, has been established policy in this state since 1901." (Hirsa v. Sup. Ct. (1981) 118 Cal.App.3d 486, 488-89; see also CCP 428.50(c).) The trial date is April 7, 2025. For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: JESUS AGUILAR SALMERON VS. AC STONE GROUP PTY LTD. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Specially Appearing Defendant Guidoni Usa Inc.'S Notice Of Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion calendar for Wednesday, September 18, 2024, Line 14. Specially Appearing Defendant Guidoni Usa Inc.'S Motion To Quash Service For Lack Of Jurisdiction. The hearing on Guidoni USA, Inc.'s "motion to quash service for lack of jurisdiction" is continued to November 25, 2024 to permit jurisdictional discovery. Any supplemental briefing shall be filed by November 8. Guidoni USA says it "has no relationship and no minimum contacts with" California. (Memo. 3:12.) Plaintiff says "Guidoni has shipped at least 34 containers of its artificial stone products directly into the Port of Long Beach, as recently as October 27, 2022." (Opp. 2:14-15.) Jurisdictional discovery is warranted. (Magnecomp Corp. v. Athene Co. (1989) 209 Cal.App.3d 526, 533.) For the 9:30 a.m. Law & Motion calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/RBU)
